systemicPatterns.pattern: Formulaic 'This is because' explanation structure that sounds robotic and unnatural
Generic filler phrases like 'since this approach doesn't match the situation' that teach nothing specific
Best answer index mismatches where 'best' field doesn't match highest rate option
Explanations contain grammar errors, fragments, or incomplete sentences
Wrong answer explanations focus on why not to do something rather than teaching the positive principle
Score perspective errors (Bot vs Top confusion affecting game context)
Scenario setup contains impossible or contradictory situations

blindSpots.description: Rate distributions that violate strategic logic (best answer having lower rate than wrong answer)
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Verify best answer always has highest rate among all options

blindSpots.description: Concept tags that don't match what the scenario actually teaches
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Validate conceptTag aligns with the primary teaching point of the scenario

blindSpots.description: Explanations that contradict the option they're explaining
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Ensure each explanation advocates for/against its corresponding option correctly

blindSpots.description: Age-inappropriate statistics or terminology for the difficulty level
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Check that language, stats, and concepts match the ageMin for each difficulty

blindSpots.description: Options that involve unrealistic player actions for the position
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Validate each option represents something that position would actually do
Verify factual accuracy of strategic and technical claims in explanations

newRules.name: Best Answer Rate Match
newRules.description: The option marked as 'best' must have the highest rate value in the rates array

newRules.name: Natural Language Flow
newRules.description: Explanations must not use formulaic transitions like 'This is because' at the start or awkwardly in the middle

newRules.name: Positive Teaching Principle
newRules.description: At least the best answer explanation must teach WHY to do something, not just why NOT to do alternatives

newRules.name: Concept Tag Alignment
newRules.description: The conceptTag must match what the scenario primarily teaches, not a tangential concept

newRules.name: Position Action Realism
newRules.description: Each option must represent an action that position would realistically take in that situation

newRules.name: Age-Appropriate Complexity
newRules.description: Statistics, terminology, and concepts must match the ageMin for the difficulty level

newRules.name: Explanation Specificity
newRules.description: Explanations must teach specific principles, not use generic phrases like 'doesn't match the situation'

newRules.name: Score Perspective Accuracy
newRules.description: Top/Bot designation must correctly reflect which team is batting and game context

newRules.name: Relay/Cutoff Role Accuracy
newRules.description: Cutoff and relay assignments must match actual baseball positioning (SS relays from left side to home)

newRules.name: Complete Sentences
newRules.description: All explanations must be complete, grammatically correct sentences without fragments

pitcher: Verify pitch selection makes sense for the count and game situation
Check that runner-control techniques (slide step, pickoffs) are used appropriately
Ensure pitch locations match strategic intent (high-and-tight for squeeze defense)
catcher: Validate pitch-calling logic aligns with batter history and game context
Check blocking/framing techniques are appropriate for runner situations
Verify defensive plays (squeeze, steal) have correct catcher positioning
firstBase: Ensure footwork and stretching mechanics are correctly described
Validate force play understanding, especially with bases loaded
Check that holding runners technique is age-appropriate
secondBase: Verify double play pivot mechanics and when to turn two alone
Check relay positioning for right-side balls (2B doesn't relay to home from RF)
Ensure force/tag play decisions are correct
shortstop: Validate cutoff positioning for left-center hits going home
Check double play feed decisions based on ball location
Ensure relay throw mechanics are properly taught
thirdBase: Verify bare-hand technique for slow rollers
Check bunt defense positioning and recovery
Validate tag application techniques for different slide types
leftField: Ensure relay throw decisions prioritize preventing runs
Check sun/wind adjustment techniques are taught
Validate backup assignments on throws to bases
centerField: Verify priority rules for fly balls are correctly applied
Check that deep position allows best angle for relay throws
rightField: Validate backup responsibilities on infield plays
Check that shallow fly mechanics favor direct throws
Ensure wall play safety is balanced with aggression
batter: Verify two-strike approach emphasizes contact over power
Check that count leverage is age-appropriately explained
Ensure situational hitting matches game context
baserunner: Validate steal timing based on pitcher/catcher/count factors
Check that first-to-third decisions include coach signals
Ensure slide decisions match base and fielder positioning
manager: Verify substitution timing considers leverage and matchups
Check that pitching changes align with times-through-order data
Ensure defensive positioning matches hitter tendencies
famous: Validate historical accuracy of the play description
Check that the teaching point emerges from the actual play
Ensure the lesson applies to modern baseball
rules: Check that examples clarify not confuse the rule
Ensure appeal play timing and mechanics are correct
counts: Validate strategic approach matches the specific count
Check that success rates/statistics are accurate
Ensure both pitcher and batter perspectives are considered

enhancedPromptAdditions:
ADDITIONAL VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS:

1. RATE-BEST ALIGNMENT: The option marked as 'best' MUST have the highest rate value. Reject if mismatched.

2. NATURAL LANGUAGE: Explanations must flow conversationally. Avoid 'This is because' structures. Write as if teaching a student face-to-face.

3. TEACH POSITIVELY: Best answer explanation must explain WHY to do it, not just why alternatives are wrong.

4. POSITION REALISM: Every option must be something that position would actually do. No pitchers involved in routine infield plays.

5. AGE-APPROPRIATE: Diff 1 (ages 6-8): simple language, no statistics. Diff 2 (ages 9-11): basic stats OK. Diff 3 (ages 12+): advanced concepts OK.

6. SPECIFIC TEACHING: Ban generic phrases like 'doesn't match the situation'. Every explanation must teach a specific principle.

7. COMPLETE SENTENCES: No fragments like 'This is because speed matters.' Must be full thoughts.

8. FACTUAL ACCURACY: Cutoff assignments, force plays, and positioning must match real baseball. SS relays left side to home, not 2B.

9. SCORE PERSPECTIVE: Bot = home team bats. Top = away team bats. Context must match.

10. CONCEPT ALIGNMENT: The conceptTag must match what the scenario primarily teaches.
